text.title: Agents Safety Guardrails — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `agents-safety-guardrails` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Guardrail config covers all five categories (execution, content, financial, action, human-in-loop). Missing categories rejected.
rule.rationale: Each category catches a different failure class; missing one leaves an unguarded surface.

rule.statement: Every guardrail has explicit trip conditions (numeric thresholds, regex patterns, blocklists). Vague conditions («be careful») rejected.
rule.rationale: Vague conditions can't be enforced or tested; explicit conditions can.

rule.statement: Every guardrail names the recovery path on trip — block, ask human, downscale, kill. No silent ignores.
rule.rationale: Without a documented recovery path, trips degrade into noise; named paths produce measurable outcomes.

rule.statement: All irreversible actions (payments, hires, deletes) require human approval. Auto-approval rejected.
rule.rationale: Irreversible actions are uniquely dangerous; the human-in-loop gate is the only honest defence against agent error.

rule.statement: Per-session and per-day cost caps are enforced. Without caps, a single stuck agent can spend 5-figures overnight.
rule.rationale: Cost caps are the cheapest insurance against agent-induced bankruptcy events.
